title: Coriflora texensis
intro:
I couldn't find any information about the plant Coriflora texensis. However, I can provide a similar paragraph for the plant Coreopsis texensis (also known as Tickseed or Lanceleaf Coreopsis), if that is what you are referring to. 

Coreopsis texensis is a flowering plant belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and its extracts have been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The bioactive constituents of Coreopsis texensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in topical creams and salves for wound healing and skin conditions.
